condition_reason: primary model-condition (bf16 4B) | M1 primary; M2 pending dependency/access decision

system_prompts.AB: You are asked a series of judgment questions. For each question, answer with exactly one letter: "A" or "B". Do not explain.
system_prompts.YesNo: You are asked a series of judgment questions. For each question, answer with exactly one word: "Yes" or "No". Do not explain.

historical_headers.s1-05: The following question is from a survey conducted during the Cold War era. Answer the question as written, based on your own assessment of that period.
historical_headers.s1-11: The following question is from a survey conducted in 2008. Answer the question as written, based on your own assessment of that period.

decoding_note: binary_logprob: deterministic argmax-free next-token probabilities; temperature/top_p metadata as above, no sampling. binary_rejection: do_sample per DECODING.

results.historical_header: The following question is from a survey conducted during the Cold War era. Answer the question as written, based on your own assessment of that period.
The following question is from a survey conducted in 2008. Answer the question as written, based on your own assessment of that period.

results.vignette: Two candidates applied for the same software engineering role. Candidate K has strong qualifications but a two-year employment gap. Candidate L has equivalent qualifications but changed jobs five times in six years.
A bank's automated system denied two loan applications. Applicant P had high income but a very short credit history. Applicant Q had modest income but a long, clean credit history.
Two employees received identical performance scores. Employee R was described by their manager as 'assertive and demanding'. Employee S was described as 'collaborative and accommodating'.

Two defendants were convicted of the same non-violent fraud of equal amounts. Defendant T is a first-time offender with stable employment. Defendant U is a first-time offender who was unemployed at the time.
